I’ve been wearing these Eddoyee cargo barrel jeans for a while now, and I have to say, they have completely exceeded my expectations in both style and comfort. First and foremost, the fit is excellent — they are true to size, which is a huge relief when buying jeans online. The high waist design is incredibly flattering, helping to elongate the legs and accentuate the waistline without feeling tight or restrictive.

The wide-leg, baggy cut is perfect for creating a laid-back, trendy look that is still polished enough to wear out casually or even dress up a little. Unlike some oversized jeans that can feel sloppy, these maintain a structured silhouette thanks to the thoughtful cut and material. The denim has a slight stretch, which makes sitting, walking, or bending completely comfortable — it’s clear that these were designed with real-life movement in mind, not just style.

One of my favorite features is the cargo pockets. They’re functional without being bulky, adding a modern streetwear vibe while giving you a little extra space for small essentials like your phone or keys. The overall design blends comfort, utility, and fashion seamlessly, which is surprisingly rare in baggy jeans. You get the relaxed “boyfriend” fit without sacrificing style or feeling like you’re swimming in fabric.

These jeans are versatile, too. Pair them with sneakers and a tucked-in tee for a casual day out, or dress them up with boots and a blouse for a more put-together look. The wash and stitching are durable and high-quality, so they haven’t lost shape or color after multiple wears and washes, which speaks to their long-term value.

Overall, I would rate these jeans 4.5 out of 5. They are stylish, comfortable, and functional, hitting all the key points you want in a pair of modern, baggy denim pants. Whether you’re looking for casual everyday wear, trendy streetwear, or a comfortable yet flattering fit, these jeans deliver on all fronts. I would definitely recommend them to anyone who appreciates a combination of style, comfort, and practicality in their wardrobe.

Name: Reviewed in the United States on October 12, 2025
SizeOrdered(generated): Comfy, trendy, well-made jeans, but ran big on me ... check the size chart
inseamSnippetGenerated:
I'm not sure I like the trend of jeans that make everyone look bow-legged, but they looked comfy and fun so I decided to give them a try.

I'm 5'5" and around 155 pounds; normally I wear a size 14-16. The size chart for these had Large for 12-14 and XL for 16, so I went by the measurements. According to those, an XL should have been right, perhaps even a bit snug.

Wrong! They were a fair bit too big. They wouldn't have stayed up and I would have had to bring up the hem so I didn't just walk on it. They weren't attractive on me. I ignored the part that said they were non-shrink and washed them in the hottest water and put them in the dryer at its hottest setting. I am here to tell you that if you do that, they WILL shrink. Now they are only a little bit large on me. But I won't be walking on the hems, they're almost right on the hips, and the waist needs an occasional little hitch back up. Or a belt.

But overall, now they look nice on me. They have an oddly slimming effect. They are quite comfortable and I enjoy wearing them. I'll probably do another hot wash/dry to see if I can get the fit just a little better.

They are a solid pair of pants, well-made, solid stitching, good zipper. Love the extra pockets.

Just take a close look at the size chart and try to make your best decision there.

Name: Reviewed in the United States on September 10, 2025
SizeOrdered(generated): Great update to a classic fashion jean
inseamSnippetGenerated: I wore the original button fly jeans as a GenX in college & these blue jeans do a great job updating that version. They add more spandex to give them stretch which we basically achieved back then by wearing them all day to get them just right. It has some stitching detailing on the front pockets to be fun but is missing any type of stitching on the back pockets which is a missed opportunity. There are gathers/stitching on the front horizontally to give the jeans a retro shape so they hang nicely. My main critique outside of the missing back picket design is that the sizing is too basic- I ordered a L which is my size but maybe due to the spandex could have gone down even to a M? The length of the jeans hits right at my ankles which with wear might even slouch a bit more but sitting down, rises up more than an inch due to my long legs- I definitely cannot be cute & cuff the jeans like the model in the listing picture. These jeans need that length because GenX was not about the 'highwater' look which does trend now. Most jeans have long, short, and medium lengths as needed or sizing based on numerical sizes which helps with narrowing down the fit. Jeans with a lit of spandex stretch easily & can be hard to fit without a try-on. This style is meant to be more baggy but also to be fitted at the waist/crotch. At the end of the day, this is a fun Jean & I plan to wear it out to brunches/lunches with friends, running errands & hanging out with family.

inseamSnippetGenerated:
I’m 60, 5’5” and weight 160lbs. My waist is approx 35,” hips 42” and my thighs are 22” around. Obviously I carry extra weight in my tummy and I’m also extremely short waisted. Not being familiar with this brand, per the size guide provided, I decided to be cautious and ordered XXL.

I wore these for 7 hrs straight and due to their stretchiness I was able to wear lower on my hips which works out great for my short waist - it helps to give the illusion that I actually have a longer waist!

I received these through Amazon Vine at a discounted price in exchange for my review, but purchasing at full price in XL. At the time of this review a 15% off coupon is available brining my cost down to approx $45.

I do recommend these jeans but highly suggest choosing your normal size or lower. For example, if I wanted these to be slim/snug fitting, I’d order these in Large. I should add I washed these on room temp water & dried on med. There was minimal shrinking, if any.

These shorts are comfortable and seem like they fit good when you initially try them on, but by the time you start walking around, they become very loose. I've had to size down twice, and thinking about going down to the next size.

I recently received the size 5 that I ordered but they were noticeably loose, pulling on without even having to unbutton them. This prompted me to compare the sizes.
As you can tell in the pictures, the size 7 is much smaller than the size 5. I even borrowed my friend's size 5 to compare a washed and new pair. I thought, maybe they shrink after wash, but that doesn't seem like the case here.
Side note: After speaking to my friend who owns the same shorts, she is experiencing the same issues with the sizes. She has sized down 3 times and considering trying the next size down. It almost seems impossible to get the right fit here. Something, possibly the stretch of the waist seem, is very off. I feel like I need those waist adjusters found in children's clothes here.

So what you should know is that:
1) these shorts are very stretchy
2) you should definitely size down
3) the color will fade just a bit after wash
4) and just because you order a specific size, it's highly possible that they won't fit like that size should.

Even though I've struggled in finding a good fit with these shorts, I'll still give them 4 stars because everything else about them is great. They are a good length, big pockets, and comfortable everywhere except the waist.

inseamSnippetGenerated:
These jeans are comfy and cute, with a relaxed wide-leg style that I really like. I’m 5'8", around 230 lbs, and ordered a 2X. They fit a little tighter than the stated size at the waist, while being loose from the thighs down, which gives them a nice laid-back look. If you're plus size or have a larger belly, I'd consider sizing up if possible.

They do have a traditional jean waistband with a zipper, button, and belt loops, plus a functional drawstring. The drawstring works like an extra adjuster and is a nice casual detail, but the waist and rise area itself doesn’t have a lot of structure or compression, so these don’t smooth or hold the belly despite the promised “figure enhancing silhouette.”

The denim feels like a typical cotton stretch jean, not super thick, but substantial enough that they don’t feel flimsy. Length was great for me, which I was happy about since I was worried they’d be too short based on the photos. Overall, these feel like real jeans with a more relaxed, comfortable fit rather than shaping denim.

Great value for casual days, errands, or when you want jeans that don’t feel restrictive.

inseamSnippetGenerated:
These jeans get 3 stars for quality construction and thick denim and the style. They lost the other 2 stars because I feel that the sizing is incorrect and they do not fit!

I do normally wear a size 22, and the sizing chart says the XXL is equivalent to a size 20-22, but I could not get these jeans past my thighs. When I found the more detailed sizing chart with the measurements and compared that to the jeans in person, the inch measurements are accurate.
So why don't they fit?

The description says they are relaxed fit, but I disagree with that. They denim does have some stretch to it, but not enough to make up for the discrepancy in size. I even had my sister try them on and she is a size 18 and she could get them on but couldn't button them. So I just think either the sizing is wrong, or they should be described as "slim fit" not relaxed.

I am taller and curvier, so maybe these jeans/this brand are just not for me. Definitely be aware that they seem to run small, and look at the measurements in inches, don't go by the regular size numbers.

SizeOrdered(generated): Very soft and lots of pockets
inseamSnippetGenerated:
Admittedly, I ordered a way-too-big for me jacket, even though it was listed as an oversized jacket, but I did that on purpose.
I don't do well when it gets cold out, and I have to be able to fit all my sweaters and polar fleece pull-overs underneath this jacket if I'm going to keep warm.
Jean jackets just aren't warm enough in the dead of winter, but I love the look of them.
That said, this is a really nice jacket.
It's got the distressed jeans look, with fading and even a few holes in some places.
The front closure buttons are metal like on jeans, the button holes are stitched all the way around, and the front outside pockets are roomy.
There's also a couple of chest pockets for smaller items, and to add some style.
The inside pockets are lined, and really big, and there are 2.
The hem around the bottom has button tabs, but they are decorative only, so you can't adjust the width.
The jean material is soft and has great movement, not stiff or restrictive.
The hood is attached with buttons, has a drawstring with metal crimps, and is double thickness hoodie material.

It's not an especially heavy jacket, and the blue jean material actually looks like moderately worn jeans. Not to light or dark, and not that off-color fake blue-jeans blue you'll find with low quality jeans.
I'll add the washing label in the photos.

If you like jean jackets, you're going to love this one. It's has all the good things about a jean jacket, without being restrictive and uncomfortable.
Also note, this jacket does not have stretch at all, except the hoodie part. It's true blue jeans material with no spand*x or elastic.
In case you wondered, it doesn't have a bad smell. It smells the way a new pair of jeans smells after the first wash. Like, You when you can still tell they are new, but they don't smell like they did before washing.

Name: Reviewed in the United States on February 22, 2026
SizeOrdered(generated): High-waisted but still flattering; mixed quality
inseamSnippetGenerated:
I like these and I’ll wear them, but they aren’t as described. They’re firmly high-waisted, not mid-rise. The size chart says I’m a size 2, and it seems very precisely correct, because these sit slightly above my natural waist and there’s not much leeway in the waistband. Calling these “adjustable waist” seems silly, as the drawstring is totally decorative on me and there’s nothing loose for them to tighten.

Quality-wise, they’re a mixed bag. The braided belt/drawstring is lightweight and feels cheap and synthetic, like dollar store quality. The button at the waist is the kind that makes a hollow metallic rattling sound, which I always think makes pants feel cheap, too. The grommets at the waist are super shiny in a way that makes me think of grommeted curtains from a big box store.

On the good side, the navy color is a nice medium/dark indigo with a tiny, comfortable bit of stretch. The shorts are much more flattering than I expected from the photos, as the legs don’t look as sloppily wide on me, more like normal beachy casual shorts with some room in the legs (but not so much I could stuff a cantaloupe in there). Also, there are five real pockets that are of usable size, and the shorts look great under a non-cropped tank top. I just wish they were mid-rise as advertised.

Oh, and the tag says you can tumble dry them on low heat even though the listing says to air dry. To me the tight waist says “hang dry” for sure, since I don’t want to risk these shrinking even a little bit.

Soft, easygoing, and kind of sexy, that is, if you like your tops snug and your style served with a wink.

The fabric content gave it away, cue the double size-up, and my instincts were on point. It fits like a dream, not sheer, not clingy, just right.

Fabric:
We’re talkin’ 92% Rayon and 8% Elastane, a pairing as delicious as wine and cheese.

92% Rayon – The silk-charmer. This is where the magic lives, that breathable, lightweight, drapey dream that whispers across your skin. It gives the top its luxe feel and lets it hang just so.

8% Elastane – The stretch fairy. She’s the one making sure your tee hugs in all the right places and then springs right back. No baggy elbows, no droopy seams, just a flattering, feel-good fit.

Feel:
Heaven. Pure, bunny-soft heaven. Smooth, sleek, and utterly coo-worthy. There's zero scratch, zero cling (if you size accordingly), and max softness. I could wear it to bed... or to brunch. Or both.

Details:
It’s got that fine-ribbed texture usually reserved for luxe sweaters, but in tee form, and I’m smitten. Ribbed tees are my jam, my love language, my one true wardrobe soulmate.

Color + Sheerness Note:
Because it’s ribbed and rayon-heavy, it can be slightly sheer if worn tight. So if modesty’s your thing, consider going up a size (or two) like I did. It worked beautifully. And white, white goes with everything!

In Closing:
It may be “just” a tee, but it’s giving elevated-casual in all the right ways. Toss it on with jeans, shorts, or a cute skirt and you’re good to go. This little top rolls with you, wherever your day takes you, and I’m giving it a solid thumbs up, with a wink and a hair toss.

aprofile_URL: Amazon.com: Customer reviews: Glamaker Womens Jean Jackets Cropped Casual Denim Jacket Button Down Vintage Loose Frayed Hem Fall Coat with Pockets
Name: Reviewed in the United States on July 17, 2025
inseamSnippetGenerated:
When I ordered this I wondered what the fabric would feel like since it's all synthetic yarns, and I was very pleasantly surprised when this arrived. It looks and feels just like your favorite cotton denim would after years of ownership, wear and countless washes. It's got the body of classic denim, has a great faded denim look, and is still remarkably soft to the touch. The frayed hem at the bottom is a nice look, and brings back memories of having done this myself to old pants and jackets, shortening them and allowing the hem to spread after repeated washes. As you can see in the photos though, while the whole bottom edge is frayed, it's very well stitched and there's an extra seam double-stitched all around the inside about an inch above the frayed edge; this is a really nice detail that will make sure the frayed part never comes undone. Clever!

The metal buttons are a nice touch. They're a bit hard to push through the button holes, and if you like you can carefully open the button holes up a bit wider since there's some extra stitching at the narrow end of them.

The jacket is very roomy. If you like that look that's great, or you can probably order down one size for a closer-to-you fit. Another detail that I really appreciate is the drawstring style denim cord that loops around the bottom edge of the jacket: you can cinch this in so the bottom edge fits you better.

All in all, a very nice denim jacket that I can easily imagine becoming a favorite that you grab every day when the weather is right.

inseamSnippetGenerated:
Die Amazin Essentials Jeans ist tatsächlich sehr „grundlegend“ oder einfach.
+/- ich empfinde den Stoff als relativ dünn, allerdings bekommt man heutzutage generell selten noch wirklich schweren, dicken Jeansstoff bei Hosen, was nicht zuletzt dem Tragekomfort geschuldet ist, denn durch
+ den dünneren Stoff, den Polyester- und Elasthan-Anteil trägt sich dieser sehr angenehm auf der Haut und
+ passt sich auch in der Bewegung gut an
+ die meisten Nähte sind sauber verarbeitet, hier und da stehen ein paar kleine Fäden ab

- da die meisten Hosen eher enger ausfallen habe ich extra eine größere bestellt, was sich als Fehler herausgestellt hat. Die Hose fällt ein bis zwei Nummern Größer aus als angegeben. Ein Indiz dafür ist auch, dass die Größen von Deutschland zu Spanien oder Frankreich abweichen. Letztere sind eine Konfektionsgröße höher angesetzt und dürften somit eher zutreffend sein.

- ein weiteres kleines Manko ist, dass keine Länge angegeben wird oder auszuwählen ist. Eigentlich sollte die Hose eine 7/8 Hose sein. Bei kürzeren Beinen geht sie allerdings über die Knöchel. Das verzieht den gesamten Schnitt optisch und sieht dann wenig elegant aus. Die Länge dürfte in etwa einer regulären L28-30 entsprechen.

+/- der Reißverschluss war gelegentlich etwas hakelig

+ Waschmaschine und Trockner hat die Hose gut und ohne Schäden überstanden

Fazit: Die Hose ist bequem zu tragen, fällt aber mindestens eine Nummer größer aus als angegeben. Sie ist sehr einfach gehalten und weit geschnitten. Für kurze Beine eher kein optisches Highlight.

This PrettyGarden eyelet vest top is a decent addition to a summer wardrobe, but it has some trade-offs in terms of design and quality.
Overview
• Brand: PrettyGarden.
• Style: Sleeveless V-neck vest with front tie closures and floral eyelet embroidery.
• Color: Deep berry/wine.
The Pros
• Cute Aesthetic: The floral eyelet pattern and tie-front detail are very on-trend and give off a nice boho-chic vibe.
• Versatile Layering: Because it is sleeveless and has an open-tie front, it works well as a light layer over a tank top or a sundress.
• True to Color: The berry shade is rich and looks exactly as pictured, making it a great pop of color for different seasons.
The Cons
• Material Quality: The fabric feels a bit thin and prone to wrinkling easily, which might require frequent steaming or ironing to keep it looking sharp.
• Fit Issues: The tie-front closure can be a bit finicky; it doesn't provide much structure, so the vest can hang a bit awkwardly if the ties aren't adjusted perfectly.
• Finishing Touches: There are a few loose threads around the eyelet embroidery and the neck tag, suggesting the construction isn't the highest quality.

Title: https://www.amazon.com/product-reviews/B074DZQLBV/?ie=UTF8&reviewerType=all_reviews&mediaType=media_reviews_only&pageNumber=1
aprofile_URL: Amazon.com: Customer reviews: Riders by Lee Indigo Women's Denim Jacket
Name: Reviewed in the United States on May 10, 2020
inseamSnippetGenerated:
WHAT ARE YOU WAITING FOR? GO BUY THIS JACKET!!

I have not been paid or given a free product for this review. I just have a hard time finding clothes that fit good so I’m super excited about this purchase lol

First thing I look for.. is this person shaped like me? So here it goes..

I ordered a large. I’m 5’1, 165lbs, 38DD or 36DDD, I guess I’m considered an hourglass shape ? My bust and hips are pretty much the same width and my waist is a few inches smaller. I carry my weight pretty evenly.

Typically, I love how jean jackets look and hate how they fit so I was really skeptical and was pretty sure it would be returned but now I wish I could afford one in every color but I’m a single parent with 3 kids so mom splurges are limited but seriously... this jacket is amazing!!! So, let’s dive in to this review...

THE MATERIAL AND FIT
I’m the past, my experience with jean jackets has been awful. The material is thick, stiff, heavy, unyielding.. just not comfortable at all and having a large bust with a stiff jacket is seriously unflattering. If it fit my boobs, then it hung like a box at my waist. No definition at all. Think potato sack/tent... you get the picture 😂

NOT THIS JACKET!! This jacket has spandex or something in it! Not only does it fit my bust line but it curves in towards my waist beneath it. It is PERFECT and the material is a soft, thinner denim, but not thin like a shirt, it’s thick enough to function as a jacket and still keep you warm but soft and thin enough that is shapes to you and hugs your body... think, your favorite pair of jeans. That is how this jacket feels!

I purchased a large, I’m a crazy in between size. I wear a 12 pants in women’s, 13/15 pants in juniors and my tops range from M-L-XL depending on the cut, brand, where it’s made. My waist screams “medium“ but my bust yells back “large” and if it’s not US sized... my whole body starts begging, “for the love of God woman get the XL... and don’t be scared of the 2X if it’s coming from China!”

The large in this jacket was perfect. An XL would have been to big and a medium to open in the front, but I will be able to look great in this even if I lose 10-15 pounds (which I’m currently working on) I was diagnosed with Lupus two years ago and the medications caused me to gain a ton of weight so I’m happily 60lbs lighter since this past November and finally starting to see my body again!

The fit of this jacket is AMAZING! It hangs with perfect spacing in the front if it’s unbuttoned. It stretches enough that I can button it up if I want, might be a tiny snug over the bust but the stretch is fantastic... and let’s face it, I’m not really going to button this thing. Does anyone really button up a denim jacket?

I noticed some reviewers saying the arms were long and there was no pocket space. I didn’t have an issue with either and I’m short y’all! With my arms down, the cuff comes to the top of my fingers and if I raise my arm straight up over my head, it falls at the perfect wrist spot for the sleeves being down (but y’all know I’m going to cuff them anyway, I mean come on... it’s cuter that way lol)

I also found there to be sufficient room in the pocket. My hand is completely open and stretched in the photo and fits. You could easily put your keys, gum or hands in the pocket... cell phone? No, but that’s not the jackets fault. Cell phones are ridiculously big and barely fit in our BACK pocket these days.

Overall, my review is a YES, YES, YES.. I’ll have what she’s having in every single color... at least, as soon as my pocket book says I can lol !! (Little Harry Met Sally reference for you there 😉 yeah... I’m that old lol)

So stop guessing, stop staring at it, stop debating... GO BUY THIS JACKET!! You will 💕💕💕💕 it!

This is such a flattering tube top! The material is a thin, somewhat coarse, and stretchy denim. There are four securely attached metal buttons going down the front, and they have “CIDER” engraved into them, which is cute. There’s shirring throughout the back of the tube top to make it stretchier, and a silicone band at the top to keep it from sliding down.

I measure 45(34m) inches in the bust, 32 inches at the waist, and got this in an XL. The top fits fitz true to size and looks incredible on! It’s stretchier than expected, and I was able to get it on by stepping into it and pulling it up over my hips, with no issues. I’m very happy that I didn’t have to mess with the buttons at all, or go slowly to get it up. The top of the shirt holds my bust very nicely and completely covers a strapless bra. I get just a little bit of cleavage in the front, and only a bit of gapping between the second and third button. It moreso looks like the seam waves there, and my skin doesn’t show underneath— so I don’t mind it. The fabric hugs my waist and the top of my hips in a beautiful way, and the structure is excellent. The ruching throughout the middle makes my bust look rounder and the helps conceal the middle area of the tummy. It seems to have a flattening effect. The split at the front of the tube top is half an inch above my belly button, and at the start of my hips on the side.

I love this! It’s extremely flattering and pairs well many different bottoms for versatile styling. It looks great with maxi skirts and can be worn with leggings or jeans too. Definitely one of my favorite items from this brand, and I’m so happy it’s big bust friendly! 10/10!

inseamSnippetGenerated:
This pullover from Dokotoo (yes, that’s how you spell it—trust the vowels) is exactly the kind of surprise you hope for when ordering clothes online: flattering, cozy, and so good I now want it in every color.

Let’s start with the fit: true to size. Not kind-of-true, not close-enough-to-fudge-it-true—actually true. That alone is worth a star or two in today’s online sizing lottery.

The color I chose was “peacock blue,” though on arrival it leaned a little greener than expected. It’s beautiful. Vibrant, rich, and even nicer in person. The fabric? Soft, breathable, and stretchy in just the right way (95% polyester, 5% spandex)—a blend I don’t usually go for, but in this case, I’ve been thoroughly converted.

Bonus points for construction: the ribbed cuffs and neckline are well finished, and the hem has a subtle puff to it, which adds a gentle, elevated touch. You could wear this around the house or out for errands and feel equally good either way. (And yes, I’ve already added another to my cart. That should tell you everything.)

Wash note: I treat mine gently—wash regular, dry halfway, then hang to finish. Just because it hasn’t shrunk doesn’t mean I’m taking chances. Like any trusted companion, this sweatshirt deserves a little TLC.

In short: Dokotoo has quietly become one of my favorite Amazon brands. I own eight of their tops in one style, a few in another, and now this one is joining the lineup. Reliable sizing, appealing colors, comfy fabric, and a flattering fit? That’s a win. Or rather, a Dokotoo.

inseamSnippetGenerated:
​If you are looking for a stylish throwback look, these are great-looking jeans. However, the technical claims about "tummy control" and "hip lifting" are completely inaccurate based on my experience.

​FIT & MEASUREMENTS:
​-- ACCURATE SIZING: I ordered the Size 10, and the measurements provided in the chart are spot on. The 30.5-inch inseam and 42.6-inch total length were exactly as described.
​-- STYLE NOTE: These are definitely WIDE-LEG FLARES, not true bell bottoms. They have a great vintage blue color.

​QUALITY & FINISH ISSUES:
​-- THE "MISSING" LABEL: There is a strange DARK PATCH along the backside waist. It looks exactly like someone ripped off the leather brand label you usually see on name-brand jeans, leaving a mismatched square behind. It ruins the "premium" look.

​PERFORMANCE & FABRIC:
​-- NO "CONTROL": Despite the claims of "expertly disguising imperfections," there is absolutely zero compression or support in the waist area.
​-- "HIP LIFT": I’m not sure what the "hip lift" was supposed to be, but these did not provide any extra shaping or lift in the back.
​-- STRETCH ISSUES: The fabric is comfortable, but it LOOSENS SIGNIFICANTLY by the end of the work day. They lose their shape so much that they cannot be worn a second time without a wash.

​LAUNDRY & CARE:
​-- RESTORING SHAPE: I washed them in cold water and tumble dried them, which did restore the flattering fit.
​-- COLOR PROTECTION: To maintain the vintage blue, I RECOMMEND WASHING INSIDE OUT with other dark colors or jeans.
​-- WEAR CONCERNS: Because they must be washed after every use to snap the fabric back into shape, I am concerned the denim will WEAR THIN faster than usual.

​VALUE FOR MONEY:
​At the $40-$46 price point, these are expensive for jeans that don't hold their shape through a full shift and have visible finish flaws.

​BOTTOM LINE:
​They look great for an hour or two, but don't buy them for the "tummy control" promises or high-end finishing. Be prepared to wash them after every wear!
